cards.title: High Weightage Areas
cards.content:
• Insurance Ombudsman (Limits, Fees, and Timelines)
• Consumer Protection Act 3-Tier System (District, State, National)
• IGMS (Integrated Grievance Management System)

cards.title: 1. IGMS (Integrated Grievance Management System)
cards.content: Launched by IRDAI to track and monitor grievance redressal in the insurance industry. It acts as a central repository for all insurance grievances in India. Customers can register complaints directly with the insurer or escalate to IRDAI.

cards.title: 2. Consumer Protection Act (3-Tier System)
cards.content:
District Commission: Up to Rs. 1 Crore.
State Commission: Rs. 1 Crore to Rs. 10 Crores.
National Commission: Exceeds Rs. 10 Crores.
Key Point: A 'Consumer' is someone who buys for personal use, NOT commercial purposes.

cards.title: 3. The Insurance Ombudsman
cards.content:
Quick, impartial settlement for life & general insurance.
Financial Limit: Max award is Rs. 30 Lakhs.
Fees: NO fee to file a complaint.
Time Limit: Within 1 year of insurer rejection.
Binding Nature: Binding on company, but policyholder can still approach consumer court.

sections.title: 🚀 Exam Pro-Tips (Repeated Themes)
items: Time limit to file with Ombudsman: 1 Year after rejection.
District Commission handles disputes up to Rs. 1 Crore.
